title: Leaving Berlin by Train: Quick Summary
items: **The middle of Berlin is shut to your train until 12 December 2026.** The Stadtbahn between Charlottenburg and Ostbahnhof is closed to regional and long-distance trains from 14 June. Zoologischer Garten and Ostbahnhof have no long-distance stop at all in that window. The S-Bahn keeps running the same tracks normally.

**At Hauptbahnhof, go down, not up.** Most long-distance trains now use the deep north-south platforms, the level Germans call *tief*, rather than the glass hall upstairs. It is four levels and a long escalator run from the street, so allow more time than the building suggests.

**Some trains no longer start at Hauptbahnhof at all.** Some ICE services to North Rhine-Westphalia and Frankfurt start or finish at Berlin-Charlottenburg. Trains to Düsseldorf and Köln run via Südkreuz. Eurocity trains to Poland use Gesundbrunnen and Lichtenberg from 25 August, and Hamburg trains cannot stop at Spandau. The station printed on your ticket is the one that counts.

**Three fares, and the difference is flexibility, not comfort.** Super Sparpreis normally starts at 17.99 euros; selected short journeys can start at 6.99 euros. It is train-bound and, outside DB's three-hour immediate-cancellation window, cannot normally be cancelled. Sparpreis from 21.99 euros is also train-bound but can be cancelled until the day before for 10 euros. Flexpreis lets you take any train that day, allows stopovers, and includes the City-Ticket for local transport.

**The Deutschlandticket is 63 euros a month and normally covers no fast train.** It is valid on RE, RB and IRE trains and all Berlin local transport, not ordinary ICE, IC or EC journeys. Since the 14 December 2025 timetable change, the former Berlin-Brandenburg long-distance exceptions are gone, so the old Elsterwerda and Cottbus Intercity shortcut no longer works. The RE8 now covers Elsterwerda to Hauptbahnhof in about 90 minutes instead.

**Seat reservations are optional in Germany.** A second-class reservation costs 5.50 euros and is not included in any second-class fare, Flexpreis included. Worth buying on a busy long route with luggage.
